codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex exec blocked by pretool-tool-policy.py hook — QA role write restriction prevents codex from writing output to /var/tmp/codex-outputs/ or /tmp/. Same block BA encountered. Self-review substituted with 5 adversarial questions.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: self-review substituted — reviewed: (1) missing doc-gen.md and security-check.md: both read-only Tier C, no file-modifying command missed; (2) count '33' factually wrong but non-blocking; (3) no non-read-only commands missed from Tier A/B; (4) do.md DONE classification defensible; (5) exhaustive survey label inaccurate but AC2 executable criterion satisfied. Verdict unchanged: pass with minor objections.

objections.title: Two commands/*.md files omitted from enumeration: doc-gen.md and security-check.md
objections.description: BA's context.json success_criteria claims 'All 33 commands/*.md files are classified into tier A, B, or C with justification.' The R2 acceptance criterion echoes 'covering all 33 commands/*.md files.' Actual file count is 37 .md files in commands/. Excluding INDEX.md and README.md (which are non-slash-command documentation files BA correctly classified as Tier C), there are 35 slash-command .md files. BA enumerated exactly 35 (30 Tier-C + 1 Tier-A + 4 Tier-B) but the count written in the spec is '33', which is factually wrong. More critically, two actual files are completely absent from all three tiers: commands/doc-gen.md and commands/security-check.md. Both have allowed-tools: [Read, Grep, Glob] frontmatter (security-check.md also includes Bash for read-only analysis), confirming they are read-only commands correctly belonging in Tier C. The ticket's Tier C table uses 'etc.' shorthand rather than a complete list, obscuring this omission. The practical impact is zero — no file-modifying command was missed, and the Tier A/B gap detection is complete — but the 'exhaustive survey' label and count claim are inaccurate.
objections.evidence: ls commands/*.md | wc -l → 37; BA enumeration covers 35 unique files; doc-gen.md and security-check.md appear in neither Tier A, B, nor C in ticket-20260517-172600.md or context-20260517-172600.json; both files confirmed read-only via allowed-tools frontmatter.
objections.proposed_fix: BA should: (1) correct the count claim from '33' to '35' (or '37' if INDEX.md and README.md are included with explicit justification for their exclusion from slash-command scope); (2) add doc-gen.md and security-check.md to Tier C with reason 'Read-only analysis/documentation generation; modifies no files'; (3) replace the 'etc.' shorthand in the ticket's Tier C table with an explicit listing of all remaining commands.

objections.title: AC2 references 'exhaustive survey above' that is demonstrably not exhaustive
objections.description: AC2 literal text: 'AND no command in commands/*.md that modifies files and lacks /push guidance is missing from Tier A or Tier B (per the exhaustive survey above).' The phrase 'per the exhaustive survey above' refers to the Tier A/B/C enumeration table in the spec. That table is not exhaustive — it omits doc-gen.md and security-check.md. The AC2's actual testable gate ('no file-modifying command missing') is satisfied because both omitted files are read-only. However, the AC2 verification recipe relies on 'the exhaustive survey above' as its evidence anchor, and that anchor is inaccurate. A future cycle reading this spec would inherit a false assurance of exhaustiveness. This is a spec-text-vs-execution-drift instance: AC2 says 'per the exhaustive survey above' but the survey cannot be used as exhaustive evidence because it is missing two entries.
objections.evidence: doc-gen.md and security-check.md absent from all tiers in both ticket and context JSON; AC2 text explicitly cites 'exhaustive survey' as basis for the 'no missing' guarantee.
objections.proposed_fix: BA should update AC2 to read: 'AND no command in commands/*.md that modifies files and lacks /push guidance is missing from Tier A or Tier B (verified by exhaustive glob of commands/*.md and classification of all 37 files).' The spec-table must be expanded to include all files before this AC can be used as a reliable verification recipe.

evidence_quality.summary: All factual claims in BA's root_cause_analysis and component_chain are backed by explicit file reads with line numbers. The evidence_type labeled 'inferred' is appropriate for a documentation gap (no runtime behavior to observe). BA read and cited: clean.md:585-591 (confirmed 'Changes already committed', no /push), dev.md:1056-1073 (confirmed /close chain), close.md:159-165 (confirmed artifact preflight requiring ticket+context+dev-report+qa-report+completion), dev-command.md:900-914 (confirmed 'Offer git commit if requested' with no /close chain). All component_chain entries have verified_via: Read with specific line ranges. The push.md push-gate token requirement is accurately described (confirmed lines 53-71). No unsubstantiated factual claims found.

scope_alignment.summary: User's verbatim requirement in Chinese: '学习dev系列commands对close -> commit/merge -> push 工作流的适配，将这个结尾工作流也加入到clean的适配。同时告诉我还有哪些command 也需要加收尾工作流的适配'. BA correctly decomposed this into R1 (clean.md adaptation) and R2 (enumeration of other commands). The /close exclusion from clean.md is architecturally justified and correctly documented: clean.md produces no dev-cycle artifacts (ticket, context, dev-report, qa-report, completion) that /close.md:159-165 requires. BA did not narrow or rename the user requirement. The three-tier (A/B/C) classification adds analytical value without shrinking scope. The Won't Have exclusions (implementing changes, adding /close to clean.md, changing inline commit behavior) are architectural constraints, not scope reductions. The user's universalist 'all commands' claim is addressed by the enumeration, though with the two-file omission noted in OBJ-1.

findings: count claim '33' is wrong; actual slash-command count is 35 (37 including INDEX.md + README.md)
investigation_completeness.summary: BA surveyed the commands/*.md directory and classified files into tiers A/B/C. The core investigation task — identifying file-modifying commands lacking /push guidance — was completed correctly: clean.md, dev-command.md, doc-sync.md, quick-commit.md, and quick-prototype.md are the correct Tier A/B candidates. However, the survey is not exhaustive: doc-gen.md and security-check.md are absent from all tiers. The ticket's Tier C table uses 'etc.' rather than listing all remaining files, which is insufficient for a spec claiming exhaustiveness. The practical impact is zero (both missing files are read-only Tier C), but the investigation was not actually exhaustive.

affected_file_accuracy.summary: All files BA identifies actually exist at the claimed paths. clean.md:585-591 contains exactly the text BA quotes. dev.md:1056-1073 contains the /update --temp and /close suggestion. close.md:159-165 contains the artifact preflight. dev-command.md:900-914 contains 'Offer git commit (if requested)'. push.md lines 53-71 confirm the push-gate token requirement. doc-sync.md ends at line 60 with 'Report changes made' and no /push mention (confirmed). quick-commit.md ends after git commit with no /push mention (confirmed). quick-prototype.md ends with 'Save as {prototype-name}.html' with no /push mention (confirmed). The do.md DONE classification is defensible: do.md line 22-26 shows explicit '/commit task-id -m' guidance after forced close. The dev.md lines 920-929 reference in Contract C is accurate (continuation spec update path).

findings: AC2 says 'per the exhaustive survey above' but the survey is missing two entries; the phrase cannot serve as reliable verification anchor
spec_text_vs_execution_drift.summary: AC2's literal verification recipe — checking against 'the exhaustive survey above' — will produce a false-complete result because the survey omits doc-gen.md and security-check.md. A verifier following AC2 literally would consult the survey, find it complete (as written), and pass. The AC2 wording needs to be updated to instruct 'glob commands/*.md and verify all files are classified' rather than deferring to the survey table as authoritative.

summary: BA's analysis is substantively correct: core gap identified accurately (clean.md lacks /push guidance), architecture exclusion of /close from clean.md is well-justified, and all Tier A/B commands with real gaps are correctly identified. Two minor defects: (1) doc-gen.md and security-check.md are absent from the enumeration, making the 'exhaustive' claim and '33 files' count inaccurate (both files are read-only Tier C, so no file-modifying command was missed); (2) AC2 references 'the exhaustive survey above' as a verification anchor, but that survey is incomplete. Neither defect affects the correctness of the dev task (clean.md adaptation + Tier A/B enumeration). Verdict: pass — BA may proceed to dev with the enumeration caveat noted and a request to fix the count and add the two missing files to Tier C.
